channel.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
channel.description:
We already have some ideas in mind about how to organize the forum
## Frontity Framework
* **Core Design & Development** Discuss core design and its development. * **Sources** Discuss the different sources we support, the source API and so on. * **Routers** Discuss the different routers we have, the router API and so on. * **Settings** Discuss the different settings extensions we have, the settings API and so on. * **WordPress plugins and themes** Discuss the Frontity WP plugin, other separate plugins, the PHP theme bridge and so on. * **Announcements** For releases and updates.
## Community organization
* **Forum** Discuss how to improve the forum organization * **Events** Inform, propose and discuss about new events related to the community.
## Content & Resources
* **Documentation** Discuss how to improve the documentation * **Articles** * **Guides and tutorials**
## Showcases
People may want to post their webs in order to inspire other Frontity users
## Unanswered/Unsolved
It may be interesting to have an easy way to find all unanswered topics where users will help to each other. This doesn't need to be a category itself, it can be accessed through the query _?max_posts=1_ for the unanswered topics and using _?solved=no_ for the unsolved topics.

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
We forgot to add a place for the documentation!

Framework Design

Get involved in the framework design and its development.

Docs and Tutorials

Discussions about how to improve the documentation or create guides and tutorials.

Development Talk

Confused? Getting a weird error? Want to share an article? Interesting memes?

Bugs

Formal bug reports of the framework, official extensions or themes go here.

Communication

Love Frontity? Let’s spread the word.

Community

Discussions about the community, this forum, events, socializing…

Showcases

Show your awesome site to the world.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Great

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
I agree with Mario

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
I feel it is a good approach. I think that when we talked to Carlos from OSW he told us that they had a lot of Slack channels but many of them were not being used by the users so they closed them. So we can start with these categories and iterate according to users demands.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
After talking with Rodrigo I think we should reduce the number of subcategories and add more later once we learn how people are using the forum. Based on that my suggestion is:

Framework Design

Get involved in the framework design and its development.

Development Talk

Confused? Getting a weird error? Got a nice article? Interesting memes?

Bugs

Formal bug reports of the framework, official extensions or themes go here.

Communication

Love Frontity? Let’s spread the word.

Community

Discussions about the community, this forum, events, socializing…

Showcases

Show your awesome site to the world.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
@orballo’s idea!

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
haha I love having a subcategory for memes

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
An iteration over Frontity Support, to embrace a wider range of conversations.

Dev Talk & Questions

bugs, questions, interesting stuff and awesome memes go here!

  • Interesting stuff and memes
  • How do I?
  • Bug reports

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
Reyes:

@SantosGuillamot I don’t understand the purpose of this subcategory. Could you explain it a little bit? As per its description, it looks like it is for posting “communication questions” - I find it quite confusing and would be more specific.

My idea was to add a subcategory for general topics about communication, questions and ideas that wouldn’t be included in Articles or Social Media. I think these questions should go in Communication category itself, but I feel users will try to fit it into a subcategory too, so maybe it is a good idea to have one for this.

Reyes:

I don’t see neither the point of having a subcategory called “About us” in the Community.

I agree with both of you that “About us” it is not the best name for it, but I would keep this category changing the name.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
SantosGuillamot:

Word of Mouth/Talk about Frontity!

I really like this change and I was about to propose the very same name than Reyes: Spread the word!
It’s much more welcoming than Events. We should find a cooler name for Articles as well

I like the idea of the About us, but I agree with Reyes, we need to find a better name because it look like the About us page of the websites too much.

And I think that if we have a category to talk about the community, we don’t need a category to talk about the Forum which is only the tool we use for the community.

  • About us => great, but we need a better name
    Talk about the community, what can be improved and the project itself

  • Forum => removed in favor of the above one
    Talk about this forum, how it works, and how we can improve it.

One last comment: I really like the one-line descriptions of the npm forum. I think they are super clear and easy to read. I’ll try to stick with one-line descriptions when possible.

I see a problem, though. In the homepage, only the descriptions of the main categories are visible. I don’t know if we can change that, but if we can’t, it’s a problem because the name is the only thing users are going to read.

It changes a lot!! So I think we should give it more priority to find better subcategory names.

Framework Design & Development

Get involved in the framework design and its development.

  • Core
  • Themes
  • Extensions
  • [WP] WordPress plugins
  • Ideas & Feature Requests

Framework Support

Have questions? Did you find a bug?

  • Questions: How do I?
  • Bug reports

Communication

Love Frontity? Let’s spread the word!

  • SELECT NAME
  • Articles
  • Social media
  • Spread the word

Community

Join the community…

  • Community Feedback & New Ideas
  • Showcases: show us your site!
  • Events
  • Random Chat

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
SantosGuillamot:

I would add a subcategory to Communication to talk about new ideas or questions about this category. Could it be Communication strategy/Roadmap?

@SantosGuillamot I don’t understand the purpose of this subcategory. Could you explain it a little bit? As per its description, it looks like it is for posting “communication questions” - I find it quite confusing and would be more specific.

I don’t see neither the point of having a subcategory called “About us” in the Community. I mean, I don’t like that name because it seems that it is a category to talk about “us”, the team behind the project and not the community.

In any case, these are my suggestions for both categories:

Communication

  • Articles
    Would you like to see more posts about a specific topic? Feel free to send your suggestions to the community, gift ideas about topics you would like to write about, or simply share your recommended reads.

  • Spread the word
    Want to talk about Frontity in your local meetup? Would you like to start an initiative to help promote the project globally? Whatever your idea is, feel free to share it with the community for support and help.

Community

  • Showcases
    Show what you have accomplished and be proud!
  • Events
    Get involved with the Frontity Community through special events.
  • Forum Feedback
    Talk about this forum, how it works, and how we can improve it.
  • Frontity Chat
    General chat about all things Frontity. Feel free to ask anything you’d like.
  • Random Chat
    A place for informal chatting and off-topic threads that don’t fit anywhere else.

Actually, I think we could leave the “Forum Feedback” as a parent category. What do you think?

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
Then, I would just change a bit Communication and Community categories.

First of all, I would add a subcategory to Communication to talk about new ideas or questions about this category. Could it be Communication strategy/Roadmap? For Community, I would add something like “About us” to talk about the community, what can be improved and the project itself.

In addition, I would change the subcategory “Communication - Events” to something similar to “Communication - Word of Mouth/Talk about Frontity!” and I would include Events as a subcategory below Community to talk about events community organizes or events users consider interesting to go to.

luisherranz:

events : yes (not for a “Frontity conference” but for people who wants to talk about Frontity in events)

I feel that if we wanted Communication - Events just for people who wants to talk about Frontity it could be confusing and we would need a place to talk about the events organized or interesting for the community.

Once we decide a better name for the subcategories, I think we would cover most of the topics, and we can always add new categories/subcategories in the future.

Communication

Better description needed

  • SELECT NAME
    Communication questions? New ideas? Bring them up here
  • Articles
    Better description needed
  • Social media
    Better description needed
  • Word of Mouth/Talk about Frontity!
    Better description needed

Community

  • About us
    Talk about the community, what can be improved and the project itself
  • Showcases
    Show what you have accomplished and be proud!
  • Forum
    Talk about this forum, how it works, and how we can improve it.
  • Events
    Better description needed
  • Random
    A place for informal chatting

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
SantosGuillamot:

I meant that it is a good idea to have a topic explaining the progress of Community, as developers are already doing in Framework Development.

Oh, sure.

SantosGuillamot:

However, I am not sure if users will try to fit the questions into a subcategory too. What do you think?

Yeah… I think that way too. The Discourse UI doesn’t make clear that you can post in a category (without subcategory) so it should be more clear than that.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
luisherranz:

Don’t you think that should go in the Framework Development category itself? If I’m a developer I don’t think I’d go to Community looking for the progress of the development.

Yes, I didn’t explain it right. I meant that it is a good idea to have a topic explaining the progress of Community, as developers are already doing in Framework Development. But I agree it is correct to continue their topic in Framework Development and start a new one in Community.

luisherranz:

What’s your suggestion for those other questions?

Thinking of this again maybe those questions should be posted inside Community / Communication (without subcategory). However, I am not sure if users will try to fit the questions into a subcategory too. What do you think?

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
SantosGuillamot:

I like the idea of having a category for Community. This could be also used to have a pinned topic explaining the progress as developers

Don’t you think that should go in the Framework Development category itself? If I’m a developer I don’t think I’d go to Community looking for the progress of the development.

SantosGuillamot:

Could be possible that users have questions not related to the framework nor the forum?

Yeah, you’re right, although we should keep one only for technical questions. The other questions (community/communication/project…) will come from a different group of people and mixing them don’t make much sense.

What’s your suggestion for those other questions?

SantosGuillamot:

Isn’t the Bug subcategory big enough to be a category itself? I am not sure about this and I would prefer to have as less parent categories as possible

I don’t think that having Bugs inside Framework Support creates confusion so let’s go with fewer parent categories as possible and we’ll see how it goes.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
I like the idea of having a category for Community. This could be also used to have a pinned topic explaining the progress as developers are doing right now.

I just have two questions:

  • I can see you propose to limit the Support category just to the Framework. Could be possible that users have questions not related to the framework nor the forum? For example, if users have questions about other community tools such as Wordpress site, future tools we may be using, or about frontity.org project (how it is organized, how to contribute, etc), where should they post them?

  • Isn’t the Bug subcategory big enough to be a category itself? I am not sure about this and I would prefer to have as less parent categories as possible, it is just to clarify it and be sure it is correct as a child of Framework Support.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Yet another iteration over this. Criticize, please

Announcements

Better description needed

  • Releases
    Better description needed

Framework Design & Development

  • Core
    Discuss core design and its development.

  • Themes
    Discuss the different themes, their APIs and so on.

  • Extensions
    Discuss the different extensions, their APIs and so on.

  • (WP emoji) WordPress plugins
    Discuss the Frontity WP plugin and other useful plugins.

  • Ideas & Feature Requests
    This is your place to bring up rough ideas and new features!

Framework Support

Better description needed

  • Questions: How do I?
    how to do this or that with Frontity!

  • Bug reports
    formal bug reports

Communication

Better description needed

  • Articles
    Better description needed

  • Social media
    Better description needed

  • Events
    Better description needed

Community

  • Showcases
    Show what you have accomplished and be proud!

  • Forum
    Talk about this forum, how it works, and how we can improve it.

  • Random
    A place for informal chatting

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: Pablo
item.description:
This is my proposal of categories that we should use in the forum, @SantosGuillamot @Reyes @luisherranz, let me know what you think. I mixed the npm.community with your requests. I’m not totally convinced with the Content and marketing subcategories, @Reyes just adjust them to your needs.

Announcements

Better description needed

  • Releases
    Better description needed

Frontity Framework

  • Core Design & Development
    Discuss core design and its development.
  • Sources
    Discuss the different sources we support, the source API and so on.
  • Routers
    Discuss the different routers we have, the router API and so on.
  • Settings
    Discuss the different settings extensions we have, the settings API and so on.
  • WordPress plugins and themes
    Discuss the Frontity WP plugin, other separate plugins, the PHP theme bridge and so on.
  • Ideas
    This is your place to bring up rough ideas and new features!

Content and Marketing

Better description needed

  • Articles
    Better description needed
  • Social media
    Better description needed
  • Events
    Better description needed

Showcases

Better description needed Show what you have accomplished and be proud!

Support

Better description needed

  • How do I?
    Better description needed
  • Troubleshooting
    Better description needed

Bugs

Better description needed

Random

Better description needed A place for informal chatting

Meta

Talk about this forum, how it works, and how we can improve it.

  • Sandbox

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: Pablo
item.description:
I agree with @Reyes Diffusion or Communication where a bit confusing, I’m okey with “Content and Marketing”.

I’ve been taking a look to the npm community forum as well and I really like their approach. I think it’s a good idea to use emojis, colors and category descriptions the same way they do.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
I am still not sure what we want to discuss in this category. Documentation, guides, and tutorials would be placed in another category (support for example) and we would discuss articles, events, social media and other ways of reaching new people?

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Ok, we can use Content and Marketing if it’s clear that it is a place to talk about how to help people discover Frontity

Any other input? @Pablo, @SantosGuillamot?

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
luisherranz:

Not the docs or tutorials themselves but the process of creating/updating them.

Oh, ok! I misunderstood concepts, sorry. I thought you also wanted this category to be a place to find related resources (e.g. docs/guides or tutorials on how to solve particular issues).

However, I still find it confusing to use a “Content and Communication/Diffusion” category to talk about what resources should we create or how to improve our docs.

Why not create a Support category like npm to include such topic and similar ones (guides of how to…, suggestions to improve docs/resources, troubleshooting)?

luisherranz:

I’m trying to find a better word for “Diffusion” that express that this is the place to talk about how to help people discover Frontity, but I didn’t find anything better yet. Ideas?

And what about Content and Marketing?

If you don’t like it either, I think we can leave it as Content & Diffusion. It’s not a bad name if we have a different site for resources.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Reyes:

I would expect to find a lot of things such as announcements, releases, informal communications amongst members, articles, social media topics, events, etc. Is this the place where we’re going to discuss all of these things?

My thoughts:

announcements: no, this will have its own category, like in npm
releases: no, this will go in announcements
informal communications amongst members: no, this will be something like “random”
articles: yes
social media: yes
events: yes (not for a “Frontity conference” but for people who wants to talk about Frontity in events)

Reyes:

I would not expect to find resources such as docs or tutorials

Not the docs or tutorials themselves but the process of creating/updating them.

What I don’t like about Content & Resources is that this won’t be a place to find resources but only to talk about what resources should we create and the process of creating them. Most resources if not all will be in the frontity.org site.

I’m trying to find a better word for “Diffusion” that express that this is the place to talk about how to help people discover Frontity, but I didn’t find anything better yet. Ideas?

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: Reyes
item.description:
I like it but the word Communication can be confusing as it has a wider meaning. The more descriptive is the name of a category the better. If I see a category called “Content & Communication” I would expect to find a lot of things such as announcements, releases, informal communications amongst members, articles, social media topics, events, etc. Is this the place where we’re going to discuss all of these things? If so, then it can be a good name. However, under the “content & communication” name I would not expect to find resources such as docs or tutorials.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?

item.title: Which categories should we use in the forum?
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
This can be useful as inspiration: https://npm.community/

I love the colours, the emojis and the category descriptions.

Read full topic
item.source: Which categories should we use in the forum?
